title: Athens
tagline: Ancient marble and modern chaos — a city that invented Western civilisation and never lets you forget it.
why_go: Walk the Acropolis and stand beside the Parthenon
World-class museums with millennia of history in one place
Fantastic vegetarian-friendly food scene and incredible street food
essential_experiences: Acropolis and Parthenon at golden hour
Day trip to Cape Sounion or the Saronic Islands or anywhere islandy
seasonality_note: Spring and autumn are ideal. July–August is brutally hot and very crowded.
food_and_drink: Excellent vegetarian options — spanakopita, gigantes, fava, horiatiki everywhere
Explore the food markets at Varvakios Agora
Ouzo, local wine, and excellent Greek coffee culture
logistics: Direct flights from major hubs; from Perth via Dubai, Singapore, or Doha
Metro from airport to city centre is easy and cheap
Walkable central city; metro and taxis for wider exploration
Wide range of accommodation from hostels to boutique hotels
friction_factors: Very crowded at major sites in summer
Long flight from Perth (~20 hours with stopover)
tips: Book Acropolis tickets online — queues are brutal in season
Stay in Koukaki or Monastiraki for best walking access
Most museums are free on the first Sunday of the month

water_safety.label: Safe to Drink
water_safety.note: Tap water is safe throughout Athens and most of Greece.

itinerary.label: Day trip — Cape Sounion or Hydra
Neighbourhood wander, food market, evening rooftop

verdict: Punches well above its weight for a city of its size. The history is genuinely awe-inspiring, the food is excellent for vegetarians, and it's far more affordable than most European capitals. A fantastic first stop on a longer trip.
